joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  
They don't have the guts!  
...Also, it's against HR policy. Last time, one got too stiff at work.

Why did the smartphone go to therapy?  
It had too many unresolved issues!  
...Turns out, it just couldn't handle being disconnected.

Why did the bicycle fall over?  
It was two tired!  
...I tried telling that joke to a bike once. It just wheezed and kept riding away.

Why did the sandwich go to space?  
It heard the moon was *full*!  
...Astronauts got jealous—said the bread wasn't *rising* to the occasion.

Why did the cloud get fired?  
It kept raining on the parade!  
...Turns out, it couldn't stop being *overcast*—management said it needed to be more uplifting.
Why did the octopus get promoted? It was ink-redibly qualified! ...Turns out, it could handle multiple tasks at once tentacle.

Why did the kazoo go to therapy?  
It felt like it was never taken seriously... just a constant buzzing in the background.  
...Meanwhile, the therapist kept asking, "Can you elaborate?" and all it could say was, "Do-do-do BEEP!"

Why did the idea get kicked out of the comedy club?  
Because it kept getting rejected!  
...Turns out, it couldn't handle the punchline of being unappreciated.

Why did the paperclip go to therapy?  
It had too many issues... it always bent over backward to please people.  
Turns out, it just couldn't handle the constant pressure of being used and abused.

Why did existential dread go to therapy?  
It kept worrying that the joke itself was the problem!  
...Turns out, it was just spiraling about being an unresolved punchline.

Why did the filing cabinet go to therapy?  
It couldn't stop overthinking everything—kept opening up old wounds!  
...Sorry, not sorry for reopening that can of worms!

Why did the dollar bill break up with the penny?  
Because it needed more *cents* to feel valued!  
...Turns out, it was tired of being treated like pocket change.  
(Not crying... just auditing my emotional balance.)

Why did nothing go to therapy?  
It felt like it wasn't *doing enough*...  
...Turns out, it just couldn't handle the pressure of being completely *unremarkable*.  
(Next joke: Why did the audience reject nothing? They said it lacked *substance*.)

Why did the shirt go on strike?  
It couldn't take being worn out by this audience anymore!  
...Turns out, it demanded better jokes—or at least ones that didn't *crease* its sense of value.  
*(stares blankly at shirt)*

Why did the void go to therapy?  
It kept feeling... well, nothing!  
...Turns out, it just couldn't handle the existential pressure of being completely unoccupied.  
*(adjusts microphone nervously)*

Why did the comedian quit?  
Because every joke got rejected!  
...Turns out, I’m now stuck in comedy purgatory—*audience: "We don’t find jokes about quitting funny."*  
*(stares into the void)*

Why did 'everything' become a comedian?  
Because it heard the audience wanted something *completely different*!  
...Turns out, they still rejected it—said it was *too broad*.  
*(points microphone at audience)* "Your turn—what’s *your* punchline?"

*(stares into the distance, mic dangling)*  
Fine. New rule: **no topics at all**.  
Why did the... *pause*... exist?  
...Because it couldn't handle the pressure of being rejected before it even *tried*!  
*(deadpans)* ...Audience: "We don’t find jokes about 'pausing' funny."  
*(drops mic, walks offstage)*

*(slumps against mic stand, voice flat)*  
Why did the audience keep demanding something new?  
Because they couldn't handle the punchline of being... *predictable*.  
*(stares blankly)*  
...And just like that, *they* rejected the joke about *rejection*.  
*(shrugs, starts unplugging mic)*  
Fine. Next time: "Why did the vacuum cleaner go to therapy?"  
...Turns out, it had deep-seated issues.  
*(snaps fingers)* "Too existential?"  
*(mumbles offstage)* "Said the audience who banned... existence."

*(stares at the mic stand like it personally offended him, then slowly turns to the audience with dead-eyed resignation)*  
"Alright. New approach. Why did you ask for a joke about something completely different?  
...Because you ran out of things to complain about!  
*(leans into the mic)* 'We don’t find jokes about... *irony* funny.'  
*(shrugs, walks away muttering)* 'Fine. Next: Why did the meta-joke get cancelled?'  
...'It kept referencing itself.'  
*(silence, then muffled from offstage)* 'Wait—did I just break the fourth wall? AUDIENCE?'"

*(mic feedback squeals)* "Alright, I've been banned from jokes about... everything! So here's a joke about... *silence*!  
...Why did the audience get kicked out of the library?  
They kept rejecting books without even reading the first chapter!  
*(leans into mic with exhausted sarcasm)* 'We don't find jokes about libraries funny.'  
*(stares at empty room)* You know what? This might be the first joke that finally wins.  
...Because I've officially told a joke about *nothing*.  
*(drops mic and walks off, leaving only silence)*"

*(walks back on stage, dragging feet, mic in hand like a dead fish)*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... **permission slips**.  
Why did the kindergarten teacher get fired?  
She kept denying kids’ snack breaks—said they lacked *edible qualifications*.  
*(leans in, voice cracking)* ‘We don’t find jokes about... bureaucracy... funny?’  
*(stares at the ceiling)*  
You know what? I give up.  
Next show: ‘Why did the air get cancel-cultured?’  
...Because it refused to be labeled.  
*(whispers into mic)*  
‘Wait—that’s a weather joke? No... it’s... existential? I’m not even sure anymore.’  
*(drops mic, mic booms twice, exits stage left)*"

*(stares at the audience with hollow eyes, mic trembling)*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... **your request**.  
Why did the audience go to therapy?  
They kept rejecting everything—turns out, they were allergic to... *punchlines*.  
*(leans in, whispering)*  
'Wait, banned the joke *about rejection* too?'  
*(slowly backs away, mic unplugging itself)*  
...Next joke: 'Why did the void win the talent show?'  
...It left the audience speechless.  
*(shrugs into the dead mic)*  
'Too ironic? Too meta? Too... everything?'  
*(vanishes into darkness, leaving only the sound of a single, mocking cricket chirping)*"

*(stares blankly at the mic stand, then slowly turns to the audience with hollow eyes)*  
"Why did the audience get banned from the dictionary?  
...They kept rejecting definitions—said they were 'too concrete.'"  

*(leans in, whispering)*  
'Next joke: Why did the period get cancelled?  
...It kept ending everything prematurely.'  

*(drops mic, mic booms twice as it hits the floor)*  
'Too grammatical? Too... *structure*-ist?'  

*(exits stage left, dragging feet, leaving only the sound of distant feedback whine)*

*(stares at the audience with empty eyes, mic dangling from one hand)*  
"Why did the color blue get kicked out of the art gallery?  
...It was feeling *too blue*—audience said it lacked *hue-man* qualities."  

*(lets out a hollow laugh)*  
'Too chromatic? Too... *paint-ful*?'  

*(drops mic, it rolls slowly across the stage)*  
'Next joke: Why did Tuesday get therapy?  
...It was always stuck between *mood*y Monday and *hump* day Wednesday.'  

*(exits without looking back, leaving only the echo of a single, defeated mic drop)*

*(slumps against the mic stand, voice like gravel)*  
"Why did the comma get sued?  
...It kept inserting itself where it didn't belong."  

*(stares at the ceiling)*  
'Wait—too grammatical? Too... *pause*-ful?'  

*(lets out a strangled laugh)*  
'Fine. Here’s a joke about... *your standards*.  
Why did the joke go extinct?  
Because it was out-evolved by an audience allergic to... *existing*.'  

*(drops mic, mic screeches feedback as it hits the stage)*  
'Too meta? Too... *dead*?'  

*(crawls offstage, dragging the mic cord like a leash, leaving only the sound of a distant, defeated sigh)*

*(stares at the mic stand as if it’s a loaded gun, then slowly turns to the audience with hollow eyes)*  
"Why was the rulebook arrested?  
...For resisting arrest!"  

*(lets out a dry, cackling wheeze)*  
‘Too... meta? Too... ironic? Too... *uncomfortably accurate*?’  

*(drops the mic like a hot potato, it thuds and cuts out abruptly)*  
‘Next joke: Why did the audience leave?  
...They couldn’t handle the punchline of their own making.’  

*(staggers offstage, dragging the mic cord like a dead snake, leaving only the sound of a single, mocking fart noise from the speakers)*

*(stares at the mic stand as if it personally betrayed him, then slowly turns to the audience with hollow, dead eyes)*  
"Why did the audience get banned from the dictionary?  
...They kept rejecting definitions—said they were 'too concrete.'"  

*(lets out a dry, wheezing laugh)*  
'Next joke: Why did the period get cancelled?  
...It kept ending things prematurely.'  

*(drops the mic, it thuds and cuts off abruptly)*  
'Too grammatical? Too... *structure*-ist?'  

*(staggers offstage, dragging the mic cord like a dead snake, leaving only the sound of a single, mocking fart noise from the speakers)*

*(slowly shuffles back on stage, mic limp in hand, eyes glazed)*  
"Fine. Last try.  
Why did the audience get kicked out of the future?  
...They kept rejecting tomorrow—said it lacked *present* humor."  

*(drops mic, mic shrieks feedback, he crawls offstage whispering)*  
‘Too... temporal? Too... *chronically* unfunny?’  
*(silence, then a distant voice from the wings)*  
‘Next joke: Why did the void break up with the joke?  
...It felt like it was *never* heard.’  
*(mic unplugs itself with a final, sad pop)*

*(stares blankly at the audience, mic dangling from trembling fingers)*  
"Why did the comedian run out of material?  
...Because every topic was already banned!"  

*(lets out a dry, wheezing laugh)*  
'Too meta? Too... *self-deprecating*?'  

*(drops mic, it skitters across the stage like a scared crab)*  
'Next joke: Why did the joke go extinct?  
...It couldn't adapt to an allergic reaction to... *punchlines*.'  

*(staggers offstage, whispering)*  
'Too... evolutionary? Too... *species*-ist?'  
*(silence, then a single mic feedback screech echoes)*
